factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices, with pricing power evident in the increased operating revenue and EBT margins.

evidence.text: SCS operating revenue increased 19% in the first quarter of 2023, due to strong revenue growth in all industry verticals primarily reflecting new business, higher volumes and increased pricing. DTS operating revenue increased 9% in the first quarter of 2023, due to increased pricing and volumes.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is successfully raising prices, leading to increased operating revenue and margins.

factors.summary: Operational efficiency is improving, as evidenced by higher fleet utilization and revenue growth.

evidence.text: SCS operating revenue increased 19% in the first quarter of 2023, due to strong revenue growth in all industry verticals primarily reflecting new business, higher volumes and increased pricing. DTS operating revenue increased 9% in the first quarter of 2023, due to increased pricing and volumes.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Operational efficiency is improving, indicated by higher fleet utilization and revenue growth.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is increasing, reflecting confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: SCS total revenue increased 10% in the first quarter of 2023, due to higher operating revenue (a non-GAAP measure excluding subcontracted transportation and fuel) partially offset by lower subcontracted transportation. SCS operating revenue increased 19% in the first quarter of 2023, due to strong revenue growth in all industry verticals primarily reflecting new business, higher volumes and increased pricing.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing capacity utilization reflects confidence in future growth.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are increasing, signaling confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: Gross capital expenditures increased to $802 million for the three months ended March 31, 2023, primarily reflecting higher planned investments in ChoiceLease.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Higher capital expenditures signal confidence in future growth.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is stable, with no significant changes in the debt-to-equity ratio.

evidence.text: Our debt to equity ratio was 211% and 216% as of March 31, 2023 and December 31, 2022, respectively.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Debt-to-equity ratio is stable, indicating no significant change.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are maintained through dividend payments, reflecting management confidence.

evidence.text: In February 2023 and 2022, our board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.62 and $0.58 per share of common stock, respectively.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Consistent dividend payments reflect management confidence.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is stable, with slight decreases offset by higher proceeds from asset sales.

evidence.text: Free cash flow (a non-GAAP measure) of $101 million for the three months ended March 31, 2023, compared to $108 million in 2022, was generally unchanged as increase in cash paid for capital expenditures was largely offset by higher proceeds from the sale of operating property and equipment.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Free cash flow is stable despite slight decreases offset by asset sales.

factors.summary: Input costs, particularly fuel and energy prices, are rising, creating margin pressure.

evidence.text: • our expectations regarding the impact of labor shortages and subcontracted transportation costs; • our expectations of the long-term residual values of revenue earning equipment, including the probability of incurring losses or having to decrease residual value estimates in the event of a potential cyclical downturn;
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — (Continued)

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing due to shortages and higher wages, creating margin pressure.

evidence.text: • our expectations regarding the impact of labor shortages and subcontracted transportation costs; • our expectations regarding the diminishing impact of prior residual value estimate changes on return on equity improvement;
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing due to shortages and higher wages, creating margin pressure.

factors.summary: The supply chain remains disrupted, particularly in the semiconductor industry, impacting production.

evidence.text: • our expectations regarding the effects of ongoing global supply chain disruptions on our business and financial results; • our expectations regarding the impact of labor shortages and subcontracted transportation costs;
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The supply chain remains disrupted, particularly in the semiconductor industry, impacting production.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include economic slowdowns, labor shortages, and supply chain disruptions.

evidence.text: • Market Conditions: Changes in general economic and financial conditions in the U.S. and worldwide leading to decreased demand for our services and products, lower profit margins, increased levels of bad debt and reduced access to credit and financial markets.; • Competition: Advances in technology may impact demand for our services or may require increased investments to remain competitive, and our customers may not be willing to accept higher prices to cover the cost of these investments.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include economic slowdowns, labor shortages, and supply chain disruptions.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include fuel prices, interest rates, and labor costs.

evidence.text: • Sudden changes in market fuel prices and fuel shortages.; • Higher prices for vehicles, diesel engines and fuel as a result of new regulations or inflationary cost pressures.; • Difficulties in attracting and retaining professional drivers, warehouse personnel and technicians due to labor shortages, which may result in higher costs to procure drivers and technicians and higher turnover rates affecting our customers.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Key sensitivities include fuel prices, interest rates, and labor costs, which pose moderate risks.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are acting as a headwind due to a slowdown and inflationary pressures.

evidence.text: • Market Conditions: Changes in general economic and financial conditions in the U.S. and worldwide leading to decreased demand for our services and products, lower profit margins, increased levels of bad debt and reduced access to credit and financial markets.; • our expectations regarding the effects of ongoing global supply chain disruptions on our business and financial results;
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Economic conditions are acting as a headwind due to a slowdown and inflationary pressures.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed, with evidence of accelerating demand in specific segments such as omnichannel retail and automotive, while there are also signs of decelerating demand due to a softening macroeconomic and freight environment.

evidence.text: SCS total revenue increased 10% in the first quarter of 2023, due to higher operating revenue (a non-GAAP measure excluding subcontracted transportation and fuel) partially offset by lower subcontracted transportation. SCS operating revenue increased 19% in the first quarter of 2023, due to strong revenue growth in all industry verticals primarily reflecting new business, higher volumes and increased pricing.
• our expectations with respect to a softening macroeconomic and freight environment;

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Demand trends are mixed, with evidence of accelerating demand in some segments and decelerating in others.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting in conflicting directions, with evidence indicating a shift toward higher-margin segments in automotive and consumer packaged goods, while also showing a shift toward lower-margin segments, particularly used vehicle sales and subcontracted transportation costs.

evidence.text: Three months ended March 31, Change 2023/2022 (Dollars in millions) 2023 2022 Three Months Omnichannel retail $ 311 $ 270 15% Automotive 254 195 30% Consumer packaged goods 218 187 17% Industrial and other 96 86 12% Subcontracted transportation and fuel 322 351 (8)% SCS total revenue $ 1,201 $ 1,089 10%
• our expectations regarding the impact of labor shortages and subcontracted transportation costs; • our expectations regarding the exit of the FMS U.K. business and the timing of such exit;

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The revenue mix is shifting in conflicting directions, indicating no clear trend.

• our expectations with respect to the effects of ongoing global supply chain disruptions on our business and financial results; • our expectations with respect to a softening macroeconomic and freight environment;

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The end-market momentum is mixed with growth in some sectors and a slowdown in others, indicating no clear directional signal.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are under pressure, with EBT decreasing due to asset impairment charges, though partially offset by revenue growth. Additionally, margins are compressing due to broader macroeconomic factors such as input costs and labor shortages.

evidence.text: SCS EBT decreased 60% in the first quarter of 2023, due to a $30 million asset impairment charge related to a customer bankruptcy. Refer to Note 2, 'Segment Reporting,' for additional discussion. The decrease was partially offset by revenue growth, primarily from automotive vertical performance.

• our expectations with respect to the effects of ongoing global supply chain disruptions on our business and financial results; • our expectations with respect to a softening macroeconomic and freight environment;
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS — (Continued)

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are under pressure due to asset impairment charges and macroeconomic factors, leading to a significant decrease in EBT.

factors.summary: Management's confidence appears mixed, with evidence of increased capital expenditures and a focus on growth, yet also cautiousness as indicated by their financial planning and expectations.

evidence.text: Gross capital expenditures increased to $802 million for the three months ended March 31, 2023, primarily reflecting higher planned investments in ChoiceLease. We believe that our operating cash flows, together with our access to the public unsecured bond market, commercial paper market and other available debt financing, will be adequate to meet our operating, investing and financing needs in the foreseeable future.

• our ability to fund all of our operating, investing and financial needs for the foreseeable future through internally generated funds and outside funding sources; • our expectations of cash flow from operating activities, free cash flow, and capital expenditures;

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Management's confidence appears mixed, with signs of growth investment but also cautiousness about financial planning and expectations.
